hero.title: Symptom Journal
hero.description: Record personal health observations, symptom patterns, severity, triggers, and follow-up notes over time without diagnosis or treatment advice.

flow.title: Record -> Review -> Discuss
steps: Create a dated symptom entry with practical details
Organize entries by category, body location, mood, and severity
Review stored history, recurring symptoms, and recent entries
Use the journal when speaking with qualified healthcare professionals

differences.title: A personal journal, not a diagnostic system
differences.description: Symptom Journal helps users keep structured notes. It does not diagnose illness, predict disease, recommend medication, recommend treatment, provide emergency guidance, or replace healthcare professionals.

items.title: Practical observations
items.description: Capture date, time, symptom title, category, severity, duration, body location, mood, temperature, triggers, relief methods, notes, and follow-up notes.

items.title: Organized history
items.description: Search, filter, sort, archive, restore, and review personal symptom records over time.

items.title: Responsible boundaries
items.description: Consult qualified healthcare professionals for medical concerns, urgent symptoms, treatment decisions, or medication questions.

feel.title: A calm health observation notebook
feel.description: The workspace keeps sensitive health notes private to the authenticated owner and easy to revisit before appointments or personal review.

items.title: Fast entry
items.description: Create symptom notes with only practical fields.

items.title: Reviewable patterns
items.description: Summaries reflect stored entries without drawing medical conclusions.

items.title: Safe language
items.description: The app avoids diagnosis, treatment, medication, emergency, or accuracy claims.

audiences.title: Who uses Symptom Journal
audiences.description: Useful for people who want a private record of health observations to remember details and prepare for professional conversations.

items.title: Adults
items.description: Track symptoms, severity, triggers, and notes over time.

items.title: Caregivers
items.description: Keep structured observations for family care conversations.

items.title: Appointment preparation
items.description: Review recent entries and recurring observations before speaking with a clinician.

resourceSection.title: Symptom journal essentials
resourceSection.description: A focused record-keeping workflow for personal observations only.

items.title: Categories
items.description: Use default organizational labels or create custom symptom categories.

items.title: Entries
items.description: Create, edit, archive, restore, delete, search, filter, and sort journal entries.

items.title: Follow-up notes
items.description: Add additional context after the original entry.

items.title: Insights
items.description: Review counts, average severity, common categories, body areas, recurring titles, and recent entries.

groupedSection.title: From observation to useful history
groupedSection.description: Use one protected workflow to keep symptom notes organized.

items.name: Symptom details
items.description: Track title, category, severity, duration, body location, mood, and optional temperature.

items.name: Context
items.description: Capture triggers, relief methods tried, notes, and follow-up notes.

items.name: Stored summaries
items.description: See totals, recent entries, recurring titles, and common body locations from saved data.

items.name: Professional care
items.description: Use the journal as a memory aid when consulting qualified healthcare professionals.

technicalSection.title: Built for private personal records
technicalSection.description: Symptom Journal follows the shared Ansiversa mini-app architecture.
items: API-driven overview metadata and generated API contracts.
No diagnosis, disease prediction, treatment recommendation, medication recommendation, emergency guidance, healthcare integration, or AI functionality.

finalCta.title: Ready to record observations?
finalCta.description: Open the journal and add the first symptom entry.
